requirements.HWF-CFG-001: syncSettings fills every unset setting from the module defaults and normalizes the field-list settings (filters, groups) supplied as a bare column name, a spec object, or an array of either, into a uniform array of specs.
requirements.HWF-CFG-002: The measure (default ALT), jaundice_uln (default 2) and baseline_tb_max (default 1) settings are settable; both thresholds are numified with a non-numeric or negative value rejected in favour of the default, and apply_tb_cohort is coerced to a boolean defaulting to true.
requirements.HWF-CFG-003: The placebo_arm and active_arms settings carry arm values rather than positions, with active_arms arrayified from a single string, and are resolved to a placebo/active side map at render time, so a change in arm ordering in the data cannot re-assign a participant's side.
requirements.HWF-CFG-004: The uln_display setting accepts band, per_subject and none, and falls back to band for any unrecognized value.
requirements.HWF-CFG-005: The summary setting accepts baseline_peak and peak, and falls back to baseline_peak for any unrecognized value.
requirements.HWF-DATA-001: Each plotted participant carries exactly one baseline value and one maximum on-treatment value for the configured measure, both in the data's absolute reporting units rather than as multiples of the upper limit of normal or of baseline.
requirements.HWF-DATA-002: The maximum on-treatment value excludes the resolved baseline record by record identity rather than by study day, so a participant whose highest recorded value is their baseline is plotted with a bar extending below the baseline trace.
requirements.HWF-DATA-003: When apply_tb_cohort is true, participants whose baseline total bilirubin exceeds baseline_tb_max are excluded from the plotted cohort, and their count is reported in an on-page note naming the baseline-bilirubin rule as the reason.
requirements.HWF-DATA-004: A participant is flagged with new-onset jaundice when their baseline total bilirubin is at or below jaundice_uln and their maximum on-treatment total bilirubin exceeds it; both clauses are evaluated, so the flag stays correct when apply_tb_cohort is turned off.
requirements.HWF-DATA-005: Participants whose arm value resolves to neither placebo nor active are excluded from the plotted cohort, and their count is reported in a note kept separate from the baseline-bilirubin exclusion note.
requirements.HWF-DATA-006: The vertical axis unit is the modal unit_col value across the plotted measure's retained records, falling back to U/L when no unit column is mapped or every unit value is blank.
requirements.HWF-DATA-007: When the plotted measure carries more than one distinct unit across the cohort, a warning naming the conflicting units is rendered and the chart is suppressed, rather than plotting mixed units on a single absolute axis.
requirements.HWF-DATA-008: The count of records dropped for a missing or non-positive reference range is reported in the notes rather than dropped silently.
requirements.HWF-ORDER-001: Placebo participants occupy the left half of the plot ordered by ascending baseline value, so the arm's lowest baseline sits at the far left edge.
requirements.HWF-ORDER-002: Active participants occupy the right half of the plot ordered by descending baseline value, so the two arms' highest baselines meet at the placebo/active boundary in the centre of the plot.
requirements.HWF-ORDER-003: Across the ordered participants the baseline series is non-decreasing over the placebo span and non-increasing over the active span, so the baseline trace is unimodal with its single peak at the arm boundary.
requirements.HWF-ORDER-004: Participants with equal baseline values are ordered by participant identifier, so repeated renders of the same cohort produce an identical participant order.
requirements.HWF-BAR-001: Each plotted participant is drawn as exactly one floating bar spanning from their baseline value to their maximum on-treatment value, with no gap between adjacent bars so the cohort reads as one continuous profile.
requirements.HWF-BAR-002: A bar extends upward from the baseline trace when the participant's maximum on-treatment value exceeds their baseline, and downward when it falls below, so direction alone distinguishes a rise from a fall.
requirements.HWF-BAR-003: A single continuous black line traces every plotted participant's baseline value across the ordered participants, drawn without point markers.
requirements.HWF-BAR-004: The baseline trace is drawn on top of the bars, so it stays visible wherever a bar crosses it.
requirements.HWF-AXIS-001: The vertical axis is in the measure's absolute reporting units — not multiples of the upper limit of normal and not multiples of baseline — so participants with different baselines remain comparable on one severity scale.
requirements.HWF-AXIS-002: The right-hand axis mirrors the left: both take their minimum and maximum from a single domain computation, and no dataset is plotted against the right-hand axis.
requirements.HWF-AXIS-003: Both axis titles name the plotted measure and its resolved unit.
requirements.HWF-AXIS-004: When every plotted participant shares one reference range the upper limit of normal is drawn as a single horizontal line; when the reference range varies it is drawn as a shaded band spanning the cohort's minimum to maximum reference value, labelled with that range.
requirements.HWF-COLOR-001: Placebo-arm bars are blue and active-arm bars are bronze, taken from a fixed semantic arm palette rather than the library's index-cycling group palette, so a change in arm ordering cannot swap the two colours.
requirements.HWF-COLOR-002: A participant flagged with new-onset jaundice is drawn green regardless of their arm, so the jaundice encoding takes precedence over the arm encoding.
requirements.HWF-COLOR-003: A vertical divider marks the placebo/active boundary, and each half is captioned with its arm name and its plotted participant count.
requirements.HWF-COLOR-004: The legend states that the jaundice colour overrides the arm colour, so the bar colours cannot be mistaken for a complete count of each arm.
requirements.HWF-BOX-001: A summary box-and-whisker panel is rendered on each flank of the main chart — placebo on the left and active on the right.
requirements.HWF-BOX-002: Each flanking panel is pinned to the same vertical minimum and maximum as the main chart, so its marks are vertically registered with the bars and directly comparable to them.
requirements.HWF-BOX-003: With summary set to baseline_peak, each flanking panel shows two boxes for its arm — the distribution of baseline values and the distribution of maximum on-treatment values — so the panel summarises the shift the bars show per participant; with summary set to peak, the maximum-on-treatment box is shown alone.
requirements.HWF-BOX-004: Box statistics — n, 5th percentile, first quartile, median, third quartile, 95th percentile and mean — are computed from the plotted cohort using R-7 interpolated quantiles and drawn by the library's shared box-and-whisker renderer rather than a second implementation.
requirements.HWF-CTRL-001: The control panel exposes the plotted measure, the jaundice threshold, the baseline-bilirubin cohort toggle, the upper-limit display mode and the summary mode, and changing any of them re-renders the chart and its flanking panels.
requirements.HWF-CTRL-002: The control panel exposes an arm-mapping control listing every arm value present in the data, so a reviewer can designate which arm is placebo and which arms are active without editing settings.
requirements.HWF-CTRL-003: Configured filters are rendered as controls that restrict the plotted cohort, with the plotted and excluded participant counts in the notes updating on every filter change.
requirements.HWF-CTRL-004: A reset control restores every settings-derived control to its configured default and re-renders.
requirements.HWF-SELECT-001: Hovering a bar shows a tooltip listing the participant identifier, arm, baseline value, maximum on-treatment value with the study day it occurred, the change from baseline in both absolute units and multiples of baseline, the participant's maximum total bilirubin in ×ULN, and their jaundice status.
requirements.HWF-SELECT-002: Clicking a bar selects that participant, highlights their bar against the rest of the cohort, and opens the participant listing.
requirements.HWF-SELECT-003: A selection dispatches a participantsSelected event on the module's root element carrying the selected participant identifiers.
requirements.HWF-API-001: The library's default export collection exposes a hepWaterfall factory that is identical to the named hepWaterfall export and returns a module instance.
requirements.HWF-API-002: The init, setData and setSettings methods replace the instance's data or settings, re-render, and return the instance.
requirements.HWF-API-003: The resize method re-measures every chart the module owns and destroy destroys them all and empties the target element, leaving no Chart.js instance behind.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-001: Every synthetic ABL- participant carries a study-day-0 baseline record for all four liver analytes, so the cohort never exercises the day-0-absent fallback path that affects 24 of 318 participants in adbds.csv.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-002: The generated file carries the same column contract as adbds.csv and every participant identifier is prefixed ABL- with a site naming the cohort synthetic.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-003: The cohort contains exactly two treatment arms, one placebo and one active, of 40 participants each.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-004: Alanine aminotransferase carries a single cohort-wide upper limit of normal of 40 U/L and a single unit string across every record.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-005: At least 55 participants have a baseline total bilirubin at or below 0.8x the upper limit of normal, and no participant falls between 0.8x and 1.0x.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-006: At least 20 participants have a baseline total bilirubin above 1x the upper limit of normal, so the Table-1 baseline-jaundice exclusion has a non-empty population to report.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-007: Exactly six active-arm and one placebo-arm participant develop new-onset jaundice, each combining a substantial ALT rise with a peak bilirubin of 2.5-4.0x the upper limit of normal.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-008: More active-arm than placebo-arm participants show a maximum on-treatment ALT below their own baseline.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-009: A participant designated as declining declines at every on-treatment visit, so the maximum on-treatment value is genuinely below baseline rather than an artefact of one visit.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-010: Baseline ALT across the plotted cohort spans 1x to 8x the upper limit of normal with no gap large enough to break the baseline trace into steps.
requirements.HWF-COHORT-011: Eight active-arm participants carry a maximum on-treatment ALT of three to six times their own baseline.
